steps.title: Focus on the claim
steps.content: Vogler's team claims that giraffes use low-pitched humming to communicate when visual signaling is not possible. The best support will connect humming to a situation in which the giraffes cannot see one another.

steps.title: Separate relevant evidence
steps.content: Choice A describes giraffes' vision but not humming. Choice C would cast doubt on whether the behavior occurs outside zoos. Choice D concerns other animals, not giraffes. Evidence that the giraffes hummed only at night when they could not see one another directly supports the claim. The answer is B.
